description: Junior with profile + roadmap exercises the mark-task → get-tasks round-trip the user hits every time they tick a checkbox on the roadmap tab. Catches regressions in the high-frequency 'mark complete' user flow.
business_rationale: Marking a task complete is the most common interactive moment on the roadmap tab — every user does it dozens of times across the application cycle. A regression in /mark-task or /get-tasks would silently break the entire feedback loop (clicks would do nothing, the checkbox state would flicker back). Synthetic monitoring of the round-trip catches both the integration bug between counselor_agent and profile_manager_v2 and any schema regressions on /get-tasks the frontend depends on.
tests: POST {counselor_agent}/mark-task with synthetic task_id returns 200 + success: true + echoes task_id and completed
Latency of /mark-task under 10s — quick interactive endpoint, not LLM
GET {counselor_agent}/get-tasks returns 200 + success: true + tasks field present (frontend renders it)
Round-trip catches the integration bug class: counselor_agent calls profile_manager_v2 /update-structured-field internally; if that hop breaks, the test fires
